title: Surinder Singh Sodhi (field hockey)

This biographical article relating to an Indian field hockey figure is a [stub](/wiki/Wikipedia:Stub "Wikipedia:Stub"). You can help Wikipedia by [adding missing information](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Surinder_Singh_Sodhi_(field_hockey)&action=edit).

(born 22 June 1957) is a former [field hockey](/wiki/Field_hockey "Field hockey") player from [India](/wiki/India "India"). He is famous for playing a major role in getting India the gold medal in the 1980 Olympic games after a gap of 16 years. He played in the center forward position.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

["India's Beijing hopes shattered"](https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/indias-beijing-hopes-shattered/articleshow/2852115.cms). *The Times of India*. 10 March 2008 – via The Economic Times - The Times of India.
[List of Goals scored by Sikh at Olympic Hockey](https://web.archive.org/web/20081204200323/http://www.sikhhockeyolympians.com/OG%20Goal%20scorers.pdf)
["Meet Top Goal-Scorers at Olympic Men's Hockey"](https://hockeypassion.in/top-goal-scorers-in-olympic-mens-hockey/). 26 August 2024.

Misra, Shubhangi (10 March 2022). ["Jalandhar Cantt's 'election Olympics' bucks Punjab trend, Congress' Pargat beats AAP's Sodhi"](https://theprint.in/politics/jalandhar-cantts-election-olympics-bucks-punjab-trend-congress-pargat-beats-aaps-sodhi/864028/). *[ThePrint](/wiki/ThePrint "ThePrint")*. Retrieved 26 June 2023.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Indian field hockey player

| Olympic medal record | | |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Men's [field hockey](/wiki/Field_hockey "Field hockey") | | |
| [Olympic Games](/wiki/Field_hockey_at_the_Summer_Olympics "Field hockey at the Summer Olympics") | | |
| Gold medal – first place | [1980 Moscow](/wiki/1980_Summer_Olympics "1980 Summer Olympics") | Team Competition |
| [Asian Games](/wiki/Asian_Games "Asian Games") | | |
| Silver medal – second place | [1978 Bangkok](/wiki/1978_Asian_Games "1978 Asian Games") | [Team](/wiki/Field_hockey_at_the_1978_Asian_Games "Field hockey at the 1978 Asian Games") |
| [Champions Trophy](/wiki/Hockey_Champions_Trophy "Hockey Champions Trophy") | | |
| Bronze medal – third place | [1982 Amstelveen](/wiki/1982_Men%27s_Hockey_Champions_Trophy "1982 Men's Hockey Champions Trophy") |  |

**Surinder Singh Sodhi** (born 22 June 1957) is a former [field hockey](/wiki/Field_hockey "Field hockey") player from [India](/wiki/India "India"). He is famous for playing a major role in getting India the gold medal in the 1980 Olympic games after a gap of 16 years. He played in the center forward position.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

In previous games, Surinder Singh Sodhi scored 5 goals against Tanzania and 4 against Cuba.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

The 15 goals he scored in the 1980 Moscow Olympics are the second-highest goals and the highest tally by an Indian in an Olympic hockey competition. It surpassed the earlier record of 14 goals set by the great [Udham Singh](/wiki/Udham_Singh_(field_hockey) "Udham Singh (field hockey)") in the 1956 Melbourne Olympics.[[3]](#cite_note-3) He was awarded [Arjuna Award](/wiki/Arjuna_Award "Arjuna Award") and [Maharaja Ranjit Singh Award](/wiki/Maharaja_Ranjit_Singh_Award "Maharaja Ranjit Singh Award") and is a former I.G Of Police (IPS Officer) and was also awarded a police medal for Meritorious Services in 1994.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

In 2022, he ran for the [Jalandhar Cantonment Assembly constituency](/wiki/Jalandhar_Cantonment_Assembly_constituency "Jalandhar Cantonment Assembly constituency") seat as the [Aam Aadmi Party](/wiki/Aam_Aadmi_Party "Aam Aadmi Party")'s candidate, losing to [Pargat Singh](/wiki/Pargat_Singh "Pargat Singh").[[5]](#cite_note-5)
